Question","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":"Question about NumPy usage or development"},{"id":1686216485,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNjg2MjE2NDg1","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/Tracking%20/%20planning","name":"Tracking / planning","color":"6ffcc8","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":21,"created_at":"2021-09-01T16:48:54Z","updated_at":"2024-09-23T21:30:05Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Feature
Hi,
I am working at PaddlePaddle(chinese DL framework). We and other DL frameworks would extremely benefit from integrated bfloat16 numpy datatype. I have seen that TF added its own implementation and lately a standalone pip package [bfloat16](https://pypi.org/project/bfloat16/) was released. 
I have also seen NEP 41, 42, 43 which from what I understand will allow adding new, user-defined datatypes. Are you guys planning to integrate bfloat16 into core numpy? If you don't have the bandwidth to do that, is there anyone that can guide me how to implement that so I can make a PR? And how are these NEPs going?

```python
if embeddings.dtype == torch.bfloat16:
    embeddings = embeddings.float()
embeddings = embeddings.numpy()
```
proper for `nn.Embedding` layers trained with mixed precision:

```python
trainer = L.Trainer(
        logger=logger,
        log_every_n_steps=1,
        # '16-true', '16-mixed', 'bf16-true', 'bf16-mixed', '32-true', '64-true', 64, 32, 16, '64', '32', '16', 'bf16')
        precision="bf16-true",
        callbacks=[checkpoint_callback],
        accelerator=accelerator,
        devices=devices,
        num_nodes=num_nodes,
        strategy=strategy,
        limit_train_batches=1.0,
        max_epochs=hparams["epochs"],
        deterministic=True
    )
```
Noteworthy, a train strategy also need the same `dtype`:

```python
strategy = FSDPStrategy(
                cpu_offload=True,
                activation_checkpointing_policy=encoder_decoder_policy,
                auto_wrap_policy=embedding_auto_wrap_policy,
                # for numerical stability mixed precision keeps the model weights in full float32 precision 
                # while casting only supported operations to lower bit precision.
                mixed_precision=MixedPrecision(param_dtype=torch.torch.bfloat16, cast_forward_inputs=True),
                process_group_backend="nccl",
                sharding_strategy="FULL_SHARD"
            )
```
